summary: Tested MIME type codec suffix fix for voice message upload. Backend: 17/17 tests pass. The root cause bug (audio/webm;codecs=opus returning 400) is fully fixed. Backend now strips codec suffix with .split(';')[0].strip().lower() before checking allowed_types. Frontend code verified: stopRecording strips codec suffix before File creation, no explicit Content-Type header on axios upload, error toast shows detailed backend message. Frontend UI: all chat elements present, audio player found in chat, 2-row layout confirmed.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix verified: backend/routes/messages.py line 54 ct = (file.content_type or '').split(';')[0].strip().lower() correctly strips codec suffix before allowed_types check
Fix verified: frontend stopRecording rawMime.split(';')[0].trim() on recorder.mimeType before creating File and Blob
Fix verified: frontend stopRecording axios.post(..., formData) - no explicit Content-Type header, lets axios/browser set multipart boundary
Fix verified: frontend handleFileUpload axios.post(..., formData) - no explicit Content-Type header
Fix verified: frontend stopRecording catch block uses err?.response?.data?.detail || err?.message for detailed error toast
Error response detail now includes actual stripped MIME type for debugging (e.g. 'Nepodporovaný formát text/plain')

success_rate.frontend: 100% - all UI elements verified, code structure correct

context_for_next_testing_agent: Voice message MIME codec suffix fix is complete and verified. Backend /api/messages/upload-media now handles: audio/webm;codecs=opus, audio/mp4;codecs=mp4a.40.2, audio/ogg;codecs=opus and all other codec-suffixed MIME types. Frontend stopRecording strips suffix before File creation. Playwright browser login via UI click was not working (session not persisting after click), but localStorage token injection worked fine - use that approach for future tests. Login credentials: test1@bloom.cz / test123.

detailed_results.FE9_no_content_type_header_stopRecording: PASS - axios.post(url, formData) no explicit Content-Type
detailed_results.FE10_no_content_type_header_handleFileUp: PASS - axios.post(url, formData) no explicit Content-Type

rca_of_issue: ROOT CAUSE: Chrome MediaRecorder.mimeType returns 'audio/webm;codecs=opus' (with codec suffix). Backend was doing exact match against allowed_types set which only contained 'audio/webm' (no codec suffix). The MIME string 'audio/webm;codecs=opus' was NOT in the allowed_types set → 400 error. FIX (backend): ct = (file.content_type or '').split(';')[0].strip().lower() strips everything after ';' before the allowed_types check. FIX (frontend): const mimeType = rawMime.split(';')[0].trim() strips codec suffix from recorder.mimeType before creating File/Blob, ensuring the File object has clean MIME type. Also removed explicit Content-Type header from axios multipart uploads so browser/axios can set correct multipart/form-data boundary.
